auth.signOutAria: Sign out of Clinical Insight workspace
auth.sessionExpiringDesc: You have been inactive. For your security, you will be logged out in 1 minute if inactivity continues.
auth.connectionErrorDesc: Unable to verify your session. Please check your connection and try again.

landing.heroSubtitle: Empower clinicians and patients with real-time risk analysis, personalized insights, and actionable prevention plans.
landing.featureRealTimeDesc: Get instant diabetes risk scores powered by clinical-grade machine learning models trained on diverse patient populations.
landing.featurePersonalizedDesc: Each assessment delivers tailored advice for both clinicians and patients, with actionable recommendations and clear explanations.
landing.featureSecureDesc: Your data stays secure with enterprise-grade encryption. Simulated 2FA and session management keep patient information protected.
landing.workflowStep1Desc: Fill in key health metrics like HbA1c, BMI, blood glucose, and lifestyle factors.
landing.workflowStep2Desc: Our ML model analyzes the data and provides a risk score with detailed factor breakdown.
landing.workflowStep3Desc: Access personalized clinician and patient advice, export reports, and track changes over time.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Join healthcare providers using Clinical Insight to deliver proactive, data-driven diabetes prevention.
landing.faq1Q: How accurate is the risk assessment?
landing.faq1A: Our model uses logistic regression trained on CDC diabetes data with high accuracy. Results should always be reviewed by a qualified healthcare professional for clinical decision-making.
landing.faq2A: Yes. All data is encrypted in transit and at rest. We use simulated 2FA, session timeouts, and never share your data with third parties.
landing.faq3A: Yes, you can export individual assessments as PDF reports and bulk data as CSV for further analysis in your preferred tools.
landing.footerCopyright: © 2026 Clinical Insight Engine. All rights reserved.

assessment.title: New Assessment
assessment.disclaimer: This assessment is for informational purposes only and does not replace professional medical advice.

history.title: Patient History
history.noResultsDesc: Try adjusting your search or filter criteria.

noAssessments.title: No Assessments Found
noAssessments.description: There are no patient assessments loaded yet. Create your first assessment to start tracking patient health trajectories.

noMatching.title: No Matching Records
noMatching.description: No patient records match the current filters. Clear the filters to return to the full assessment history, or create a new assessment if this patient needs fresh data.

import.title: Bulk Import CSV
import.instructions: Upload a CSV file with patient data to perform batch assessments.
import.dropZone: Drop your CSV file here or click to browse

notFound.title: 404 - Page Not Found
notFound.message: The page you are looking for does not exist.

low.clinician: Monitor annually. No immediate intervention required.
low.patient: Keep up the good work! Continue your healthy lifestyle and routine checkups.

moderate.clinician: Recommend lifestyle counseling. Repeat HbA1c in 6 months.
moderate.patient: Consider increasing physical activity and managing your diet to lower your risk.

high.clinician: High risk detected. Refer for diagnostic testing and consider intervention.
high.patient: Please consult your doctor soon to discuss a detailed prevention plan.

hba1c.clinician: Review glycemic control and consider initiating or adjusting therapy.
hba1c.patient: Focus on managing your blood sugar through diet and prescribed medications.

glucose.clinician: Immediate follow-up on elevated glucose levels may be necessary.
glucose.patient: Monitor your daily glucose readings closely and follow your meal plan.

bmi.clinician: Discuss weight management strategies and nutritional counseling.
bmi.patient: Work on achieving a healthier weight through balanced nutrition and regular exercise.

hypertension.clinician: Optimize blood pressure management and monitor for complications.
hypertension.patient: Regularly check your blood pressure and reduce salt intake.

smoking.clinician: Provide smoking cessation resources and support.
smoking.patient: Quitting smoking is one of the most effective ways to reduce your diabetes risk.

heartDisease.clinician: Coordinate care with cardiology and manage cardiovascular risk factors.
heartDisease.patient: Manage your heart health as it is closely linked to diabetes risk.

age.clinician: Consider age-related metabolic changes in the management plan.
age.patient: As you get older, it's more important to stay active and monitor your health.

emptyState.title: No Assessments Yet
emptyState.description: Your statistics will appear here once you run your first risk assessment.

emptyAssessments.title: No Assessments Yet
emptyAssessments.description: Your completed risk assessments will appear here after your care team shares results with this patient account.

emptyTrends.descriptionOne: At least two completed assessments are needed before a risk trend chart can be drawn.
emptyTrends.descriptionNone: Trend charts will appear here once assessment results are available for this account.

myHealth.title: My Health Portal
myHealth.fallbackAdviceHigh: Please schedule an appointment with your clinician to check diagnostic lab ranges.
myHealth.fallbackAdviceModerate: Making positive dietary changes and staying active helps lower type 2 diabetes risk.
myHealth.fallbackAdviceLow: Continue maintaining a healthy, balanced lifestyle and regular physical activity.

patientResult.basedOnInfo: Based on your provided information, your preventive diabetes risk is considered
patientResult.progressBadgesDesc: See improvements and long-term trends based on this assessment and past history.
patientResult.clinicianViewDesc: This view keeps the quantitative score, model confidence, contributing factors, and follow-up actions together for clinical review.
patientResult.noRiskDriving: No risk-driving factors were highlighted by the model.
patientResult.noProtectiveSignals: No lower-risk signals were highlighted by the model.
patientResult.explainabilityDesc: Relative contribution is scaled from the model's returned factor ranking for this assessment.
patientResult.pdfError: Unable to export the PDF report. Please try again.
patientResult.guidance1: Review these results with a qualified clinician before making medical decisions.
patientResult.guidance2: Focus first on the highlighted risk factors that can be changed through care planning.
patientResult.guidance3: Track BMI, HbA1c, and blood glucose over time so future assessments have context.
patientResult.clinicianAction1: Confirm risk category against the patient's full history and current medication profile.
patientResult.clinicianAction2: Use the factor breakdown to prioritize follow-up labs, counselling, or referrals.
patientResult.clinicianAction3: Compare this assessment with prior visits to identify meaningful trajectory changes.

patientPresentation.title: Patient Presentation Mode
patientPresentation.basedOnInfo: Based on your provided information, your preventive diabetes risk is considered
patientPresentation.targetHba1cDesc: Entering the pre-diabetes regression range by optimizing metabolic control.
patientPresentation.targetBmiDesc: A 5% body weight reduction significantly improves insulin sensitivity and cardiovascular health.
patientPresentation.maintainHealthDesc: Keep up the great work! Continue with routine physical activity and balanced nutrition.

predictionExplanation.noPositiveContributors: No strong positive contributors were identified.

simulator.adjustValues: Adjust values above to see the impact.
simulator.simulationReady: Your what-if risk preview is ready.

healthBadges.title: Health improvement badges
healthBadges.emptyDesc: Keep tracking patient assessments to start earning improvement badges. Badges appear when metrics or overall risk trend better compared to previous evaluations.

copilot.title: Clinical Copilot
copilot.noRecommendationsDesc: The current assessment profile does not trigger any actionable suggestions.
copilot.summaryLine: {gender}, age {age}, BMI {bmi}{hypertension}{heartDisease}, smoking history: {smoking}. HbA1c: {hba1c}%, blood glucose: {glucose} mg/dL. Risk category: {rc}.

factorReasoning.age: Risk changes with age because blood vessels and metabolic control can become less resilient over time.
factorReasoning.bmi: BMI helps estimate weight-related strain that can influence blood pressure, insulin resistance, and heart workload.
factorReasoning.hba1c: HbA1c reflects longer-term blood sugar control, so higher values can point to sustained metabolic stress.
factorReasoning.glucose: Blood glucose shows the current sugar level, which can reinforce or soften the overall diabetes risk signal.
factorReasoning.hypertension: High blood pressure increases cardiovascular strain and can raise the chance of future heart complications.
factorReasoning.heartDisease: Prior heart disease is a strong clinical history marker and usually increases baseline cardiovascular risk.
factorReasoning.smoking: Smoking history affects blood vessels and inflammation, so current or past exposure can shift risk upward.
factorReasoning.gender: Sex-linked population patterns can slightly shift the model's baseline risk estimate.
